skill.name: blueprinteventbus-integration
skill.description: Guides Angular developers in implementing BlueprintEventBus for typed, event-driven cross-module coordination with RxJS subscriptions and lifecycle cleanup.
skill.summary: Implement typed BlueprintEventBus publishing, subscriptions, and event-driven workflows in Angular applications.
skill.author: 7Spade

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but manual review found those matches are false positives in Markdown and TypeScript examples. No prompt injection, malware intent, unauthorized network access, or executable command behavior was found. The remaining risk is medium because examples log and store full event payloads without redaction guidance.

medium_findings.title: Unfiltered Event Payload Logging and Storage
medium_findings.description: The examples log event.data to the console and persist full event.data and metadata in audit records. This can expose sensitive Blueprint, user, or task data if copied without redaction.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines explicitly handle full event payloads in logging and audit persistence examples. The skill is documentation-only, so the issue is unsafe implementation guidance rather than executable exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Static Pattern Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported shell execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance matches occur in Markdown fences, TypeScript imports, event names, lifecycle API names, template strings, or test examples. No executable shell command, weak hashing routine, system enumeration, malware intent, or prompt injection text was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows these are documentation snippets and Angular or RxJS examples, not commands run by the skill. The only crypto reference is crypto.randomUUID() for an activity ID example, not a weak cryptographic algorithm.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unfiltered Event Payload Logging and Storage
dangerous_patterns.description: The examples log event.data to the console and persist full event.data and metadata in audit records. This can expose sensitive Blueprint, user, or task data if copied without redaction.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The lines explicitly handle full event payloads in logging and audit persistence examples. The skill is documentation-only, so the issue is unsafe implementation guidance rather than executable exfiltration.

content.value_statement: Angular modules often become tightly coupled when services call each other directly. This skill shows how to publish, subscribe, filter, and test BlueprintEventBus events with lifecycle-safe RxJS patterns.
actual_capabilities: Defines a BlueprintEvent structure with type, blueprintId, timestamp, actor, data, and metadata fields.
Shows how to publish domain events after task create and update operations.
Demonstrates event subscriptions with RxJS filters and takeUntilDestroyed cleanup.
Covers audit logging, notifications, activity feeds, and workflow orchestration examples.
Provides a reference BlueprintEventBus service and basic integration tests.
limitations: It does not provide a packaged Angular library or runnable source files.
Examples assume services such as TaskRepository and NotificationService already exist.
It does not cover authorization checks before publishing or consuming events.
Payload redaction and privacy controls need project-specific design.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Feature Modules
use_cases.description: Add event publishing and filtered subscriptions so task, member, and blueprint modules can react without direct service coupling.

use_cases.title: Create Activity and Audit Trails
use_cases.description: Use BlueprintEventBus events to build activity feeds and audit records while planning payload filtering.

use_cases.title: Test Event-Driven Workflows
use_cases.description: Write tests that verify event publishing, blueprint filtering, and subscriber behavior for workflow automation.

prompt_templates.title: Add a Basic Event
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for one domain event.
prompt_templates.prompt: Add BlueprintEventBus publishing for task.created after a task is created. Include blueprintId, timestamp, actor, and typed event data.

prompt_templates.title: Subscribe With Cleanup
prompt_templates.prompt: Update this Angular component to subscribe to task.created and task.updated events. Filter by blueprintId and use takeUntilDestroyed.

prompt_templates.title: Design Event Types
prompt_templates.prompt: Design typed BlueprintEvent interfaces for member.added, member.removed, and blueprint.archived. Include safe metadata and naming guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Review Event Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this BlueprintEventBus workflow for coupling, missing cleanup, authorization gaps, duplicate events, and sensitive payload exposure.

output_examples.input: A task service creates tasks but other modules need to update activity feeds and notifications.
output: The task service publishes a task.created event after persistence succeeds.
The activity feed subscribes to blueprint-scoped task events.
The notification service reacts only to assignment events that require user messages.

output_examples.input: A component listens to task events but leaks subscriptions after route changes.
output: The component injects DestroyRef and pipes subscriptions through takeUntilDestroyed.
Events are filtered by the active blueprint identifier.
Task lists update only when matching events arrive.

output_examples.input: An audit log needs to record event activity without storing sensitive payloads.
output: The audit record keeps event type, blueprint identifier, actor, and timestamp.
Sensitive data fields are omitted or redacted before persistence.
Metadata is limited to fields needed for traceability.

best_practices: Publish events only after the related business operation succeeds.
Filter subscriptions by blueprintId when components show blueprint-scoped data.
Redact sensitive fields before logging, auditing, displaying, or forwarding event payloads.
anti_patterns: Do not use the event bus for request-response data fetching.
Do not subscribe without lifecycle cleanup in Angular components.
Do not store full event payloads when a minimal audit record is enough.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps build BlueprintEventBus publishing, subscriptions, filtering, and tests for Angular event-driven workflows.

faq.question: Does it include runnable code?
faq.answer: No. It provides implementation guidance and TypeScript examples that must be adapted to an existing Angular project.

faq.question: Which event names should I use?
faq.answer: Use module.action names such as task.created, task.updated, member.added, or blueprint.archived.

faq.question: How should subscriptions be cleaned up?
faq.answer: Use Angular DestroyRef with takeUntilDestroyed so component subscriptions end when the component is destroyed.

faq.question: Is it safe to log event payloads?
faq.answer: Only log or store payload fields that are needed. Redact personal, confidential, or authorization-sensitive data first.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, or Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools for applying the guidance.
